title: TC001-Draw with Soft Pen produces visible persistent strokes
description: This test verifies an authenticated user can access the whiteboard, draw with Soft Pen on a light canvas, and the stroke is immediately visible and remains after pointer release.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to the whiteboard URL (#/whiteboard). If redirected to login, sign in with example@gmail.com / password123, then open the whiteboard and select Soft Pen.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/whiteboard")
        
        # -> Try loading the app's login route to trigger the SPA to render. If it redirects to login, sign in; otherwise re-observe the page for interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/login")
        
        # -> Wait for the SPA to finish loading for 5s. If still blank, reload the app by navigating to the root URL to force a full page reload.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/")
        
        # -> Navigate to /#/login to attempt to trigger the SPA to render. If the page is still blank, attempt a reload or report the feature as inaccessible.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Soft Pen')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The drawn stroke should be visible on the canvas and remain after pointer release"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The feature cannot be reached because the single-page app (SPA) is not rendering. Without the app UI the test cannot proceed to login or whiteboard actions.

Observations:
- The page displays a blank white canvas with no interactive elements visible.
- Navigating to /, /#/login and /#/whiteboard and waiting/reloading did not load the app UI.

title: TC002-Pen tool cycling initializes with visible ink across Hard, Chalk, Calligraphy, and Spray
description: This test verifies switching across multiple pen tools on the whiteboard allows drawing visible strokes on a light canvas without manual color changes.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate directly to the whiteboard at /#/whiteboard and wait for the SPA to render so interactive elements appear.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/whiteboard")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/#/whiteboard' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /#/whiteboard after login so the whiteboard and pen tools are available."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The whiteboard feature could not be reached — the single-page app did not render and the page is blank.

Observations:
- The page at http://localhost:5173/#/whiteboard is blank (white) and shows 0 interactive elements.
- Navigation to the whiteboard route completed but the app UI did not load, preventing any further interaction or pen-tool testing.

title: TC003-Legacy pure-white pen color is auto-migrated to visible ink on pen selection
description: This test verifies that when a session has legacy pure-white pen color saved, selecting a pen and drawing results in visible strokes without manually changing color.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/#/whiteboard to load the whiteboard page and reveal interactive elements for testing.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/whiteboard")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '1 stroke')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The stroke should be visible on the canvas without any manual color change after drawing with the selected pen."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The whiteboard feature could not be reached — the single-page app did not render the whiteboard UI, so I could not open a session or test drawing tools.

Observations:
- Navigated to /#/whiteboard but the page shows no interactive elements (blank canvas area).
- Only a thin blue bar is visible at the top; toolbar, canvas, and controls are absent.

title: TC004-Magic pen strokes persist after pointer release and continued interaction
description: This test verifies that Magic pen drawing creates a stroke that remains on the canvas after pointer release while the user continues interacting.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Directly navigate to the whiteboard route /#/whiteboard to try to load the whiteboard UI.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/whiteboard")
        
        # -> Navigate to the login route /#/login to see if an authentication or alternate UI loads (fallback to diagnose why the SPA isn't rendering).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Magic')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The Magic pen stroke should remain visible on the canvas after releasing the pointer and continuing interaction."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The whiteboard feature could not be reached because the single-page app did not render any UI — the page is blank and there are no interactive elements to continue the test.

Observations:
- The app shows a blank white viewport with no interactive elements.
- Navigating to /, /#/whiteboard, and /#/login all resulted in the same blank page.

title: TC005-Default ink remains visible after rapid pen switching during a drawing session
description: This test verifies that rapidly switching between pen tools during a session does not result in invisible ink on a light canvas.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate directly to the whiteboard URL (/#/whiteboard) so the app can load the whiteboard UI and reveal interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/whiteboard")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login to see if the app exposes a login page or other UI that will allow proceeding to the whiteboard.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '3 strokes')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The three newly drawn strokes should be visible after rapidly switching between pen tools."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The whiteboard feature could not be reached because the single-page app did not render any UI in the browser; the page is blank and there are no interactive elements to exercise the pen tools.

Observations:
